title: tigridia grandiflora
intro: Tigridia grandiflora, also known as the Mexican shell flower, is a perennial flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant's tubers and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and supporting the immune system. Tigridia grandiflora can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk medicine in combination with other herbs.
